vibe: Objective, rigid, highly functional, academic authority.

rules.general: Never use Inter for headings. Use radical weight contrast (Black vs Light).

rules.contrast: Minimum 4.5:1 for body text. Use pure black/white contrast for primary structural elements.
rules.accents: Color is Function Only. 95% of the UI should be monochrome. Use the accent Blue (#2563EB) strictly for active states, primary selections, and highlights.
rules.hover: Always explicitly set hover states with higher contrast (e.g., hover:bg-black/90).

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Generous Spacing for landing pages, High-Density 'Control Room' for the app/dashboard.

landing_page.grid: Bento Grid (Mode A: The Tetris Grid). grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-12 gap-8.
landing_page.spacing: Massive paddings (py-24 to py-32) between sections.

app_dashboard.grid: Control Room Grid (Mode B). grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4 or gap-6. Strict hierarchy.
app_dashboard.spacing: Tight padding (p-4 to p-6) inside widgets.

surfaces.cards: Flat background, 1px subtle border (border-slate-200), no shadow by default. Sharp edges (rounded-sm or max rounded-md).
surfaces.headers: Solid background with a hard bottom border for the app. Glassmorphism (bg-white/70 backdrop-blur-xl border-b border-slate-200/50) for landing page sticky nav.

inputs_and_forms.style: Sharp, technical borders. focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent focus:border-accent. No soft shadows.
inputs_and_forms.labels: Clear, uppercase tracking-wider labels for form fields.

chat_interface.layout: Progressive disclosure. Chat takes center stage with a collapsible right-sidebar for settings.
chat_interface.bubbles: AI bubbles: surface background (white) with subtle border. User bubbles: pale gray (muted) background, no border. Sharp corners on the flush side, rounded on others.

buttons.primary: bg-primary text-primary_foreground hover:bg-primary/90 rounded-sm font-semibold tracking-wide

images.description: Minimalist high-end 3D abstract illustration of a glowing graduation cap floating above a neural network. Use for landing page hero.
3D rendering of a futuristic textbook with glowing holographic diagrams. Use for the 'Diagram/Image Generator' feature showcase on landing page.
Students studying with a laptop. Use for social proof / about section on the landing page.
Bright modern minimalist library. Use as a subtle backdrop or for the 'Question Paper Generator' feature section.

icons.usage: Use crisp, uncluttered icons. Stroke width 1.5 or 2.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. IMPLEMENTATION STRATEGY: Build a marketing landing page AND the core SaaS application interface (Chat, Paper Generator, Diagram Generator).
2. LANDING PAGE: Use a Bento Grid layout with massive typography for headers (Cabinet Grotesk). Highlight the AI Chat and Generator features prominently using the provided generated media URLs.

3. SAAS INTERFACE: Implement a dense, 'Control Room' layout for the app. The 'Education Selector Bar' (Standard, Subject, Syllabus, etc.) must be prominent, either as a sticky top bar or a left sidebar filter panel.

4. COMPONENTS: heavily utilize shadcn/ui components but customize them to fit the Swiss & High-Contrast archetype. Reduce border-radius to max 4px (rounded-sm) for a sharp, technical look. Use 1px borders aggressively to separate sections.
5. CHAT UI: Build a ChatGPT-style interface with a focus on academic context. Ensure code blocks, tables, and long-form text are highly readable (use IBM Plex Sans).
6. GENERATORS: For the Question Paper and Diagram generators, create distinct forms utilizing grid layouts. Ensure there are 'Preview' areas (PDF preview, Image gallery) alongside the forms.
7. ASSETS: NEVER use placeholder image URLs. STRICTLY use the provided image URLs in the media section. Images must be contained properly without breaking mobile viewports (max-h-[80vh]).
8. TESTING: Apply 'data-testid' to ALL interactive elements (buttons, inputs, dropdowns) using kebab-case role-based naming (e.g., 'generate-paper-button').
9. FONTS: Import 'Cabinet Grotesk', 'IBM Plex Sans', and 'JetBrains Mono' from Google Fonts or equivalent CDN in the global CSS/HTML header.
